THOUGHT:
The current citation list only contains Sambasivan et al. (2021), which addresses data-centric failures. To build a comprehensive foundation for an ICLR workshop paper on real-world deep learning pitfalls, I need to systematically cover the remaining citation categories. The most critical missing piece is a foundational work that explains why models degrade in production despite strong academic performance. A paper on system-level challenges and "hidden technical debt" in ML will anchor the discussion on infrastructure, monitoring, and data pipeline failures. This citation will simultaneously cover multiple required categories: introducing specific concepts/theories of ML system degradation (2), comparing lab benchmarks vs. production reality (4), highlighting research gaps in MLOps and robust evaluation (5), referencing established methodologies for sustainable deployment (6), and supporting arguments about the need for holistic system design (7).
